english: — z̀u-glén (zhu gleng), z̀u-glén byéd-pa (zhu gleng byed pa) to address, accost, resp. C. — z̀u-rgyá (zhu rgya) (v. rgya-ma (rgya ma)) 1. petitionary letter, petition, suit. 2. any writing addressed to superiors. — z̀u-rgyú (zhu rgyu) the subject of a petition or suit. — *z̀u-ṅó-pa* (*zhu ngo pa*), C., intercessor, advocate, mediator, *z̀u-ṅó jhé-pa* (*zhu ngo jhe pa*) to intercede, to advocate. — z̀u-rjés (zhu rjes) 1. the chyme mixed with bile (cf. ma-z̀u (ma zhu) above). 2. the place of it, z̀u-rjés na (zhu rjes na) I feel a pain there Med. 3. eructation, rising, ká-la zu-rjés skyur (ka la zu rjes skyur) S.g. caused by beer; ro daṅ z̀u-rjés mṅár-mo (ro dang zhu rjes mngar mo) Med. a sweetish taste and rising (from the stomach). — z̀u-rten (zhu rten) the present which, according to oriental notions, has necessarily to attend or introduce a petition. — z̀u-dón (zhu don) prop. drift, subject of a petition; in a general sense = źu-ba (zhu ba) request, suit, address, communication etc. — z̀u-sná (zhu sna) (pronounced *z̀u-ná* (*zhu na*)) W. = z̀u-ṅó (zhu ngo). — źu-po (zhu po), źu-ba-po (zhu ba po) = źu-mk'an (zhu mkhan) 1. — źu-byéd (zhu byed) v. above — źu-búl (zhu bul), pronounced *źum-búl* (*zhum bul*), petitioning, making a suit in an humble posture with folded hands Cs. — źu-yíg (zhu yig), źu-śóg (zhu shog), źu-bai p'rín-yíg (zhu ba'i phrin yig) a petition, źu-yíg-gi rten (zhu yig gi rten) = źu-rten (zhu rten). — źu-lán (zhu lan) answer to a petition. — źu-lóg (zhu log) a feigned, false, designing suit, *gyáb-pa* (*gyab pa*) to address such a one C.
resp. fire, e.g. the fire lighted for cremation Tar. 7, 4.
with byéd-pa (byed pa) to nod or bow repeatedly, of a pigeon Mil.

1. to twine, to twist W. *źúd-će* (*zhud ce*), *źú-će* (*zhu ce*). — 2. to spin Cs., z̀u-kór (zhu kor) spindle, distaff. — 3. to rub Cs. — 4. to hang up, to suspend Ts. — źud-tág (zhud tag) = dpyaṅ-tág (dpyang tag).

melted Cs.; *źun táṅ-će* (*zhun tang ce*) W. to melt, trs ; źun-tár byéd-pa (zhun tar byed pa) to melt and beat to pieces Mil.; źun-tigs (zhun tigs) spark flying from red-hot iron W.; źun-ma (zhun ma) that which is melted, gser sogs źun-mai ṕuṅ-po (gser sogs zhun ma'i phung po) heaps of melted gold and other metals Glr. — źun-mar (zhun mar) v. mar (mar). — źun-mo (zhun mo) melted, whatever melts easily Cs. (who spells it bźun-mo (bzhun mo)).

1. sbst. fear, dismay, despondency, faint-heartedness, sems źum-na (sems zhum na) if I continue undismayed Dzl.; dkon-mćóg ysúm-la źum-pa-med-par bkúr-bsti byéd-pa (dkon mchog gsum la zhum pa med par bkur bsti byed pa) to honour the three most Precious undauntedly, with a cheerful heart; sems ráb-tu źum-par gyúr-to (sems rab tu zhum par gyur to) they became greatly dejected in mind, their spirits were much cast down Pth. — 2. vb. *lbú-wa źum-će* (*lbu wa zhum ce*) W. to scum, to skim (off).
1. snout, muzzle, trunk. — 2. sup. of źu-ba (zhu ba).
Ts.: *źū-źū jhé'-pa* (*zhu zhu jhe pa*) to stroke, to caress.
v. z̀u-dág (zhu dag), ཞུས་པ་ źus-pa (zhus pa) v. źu-ba (zhu ba).

(cf. źen (zhen)) 1. inclination, affection, heart, mind; volition; there is a proverb in C.: *mi k'á-pó-ćé-la źe me', ću nyóg-po-ćé-la tiṅ me'* (*mi kha po che la zhe me, chu nyog po che la ting me*) a braggart has no mind, as muddy water has no bottom, i.e. as in muddy water you cannot see the bottom, so you cannot rely on the solid principles of a braggart; k'a-źé (kha zhe) v. k'a (kha), comp.; źe bkon-pa (zhe bkon pa) or °k'on-pa (khon pa) a hating mind, rkám-pa (rkam pa) Sch. a covetous, tág pa (tag pa) Mil. a sincere, nág-po (nag po) C. a wicked, ytsáṅ-ba (gtsang ba) Sch. a pure heart or mind, or also hating, covetous etc. as to mind (several other combinations of this kind, given by Sch., are too doubtful to be copied); źe-ycód-pa (zhe gcod pa) Sch.: 'to lose courage, to have no longer any inclination for', perh. better, to resign, and źe-bcád (zhe bcad) resignation, as a Buddhist virtue Mil.; on the other hand, źe ycód-pai tsig (zhe gcod pa'i tshig) Sch.: 'slanderous words' which, e.g. Dzl. 22, 11, well agrees with the context, but is not clear in point of etymology. — źe-dúg (zhe dug) damage, destruction Sch., byéd-pa (byed pa) to cause, to inflict. — źe-lóg (zhe log) v. źen-lóg (zhen log) sub źen-pa (zhen pa). — źe-sún (zhe sun) angry, cross, ill-humoured, vexed. — 2. numerical word for bźi-bću (bzhi bcu) in the abridged numbers źe-ycíg (zhe gcig) etc., 41 to 49. — 3. numerical figure: 111.
rarely źés-na (zhes na), v. ćé-na (ce na).
reverence, respect, civility, politeness, źe-sa daṅ bcás-pa (zhe sa dang bcas pa) reverential, respectful Pth.; deí dús-su mís p'yag daṅ (de'i dus su mis phyag dang)
